'''Jason Roach''' (born {{birth date|1971|05|02|df=y}}), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Roachy"''', or  '''"Rooster"''', is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}} in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and at club level for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Swinton Lions]] (three spells), [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Warrington Wolves]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Barrow Raiders]] and the [[Blackpool Panthers]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Jason Roach was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], [[Lancashire]], England.

==International honours==
Jason Roach won 8 caps for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] in 1998–2004 while at Warrington and Swinton.<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 106–107}}</ref> Scoring two tries on his début in Paris.
